name: BrowserStack
routeName: Selenium Node.js sample build on Automate
firstSuccessOutcome: A sample Selenium build runs on BrowserStack and its results appear on the Automate dashboard.

steps.action: Open BrowserStack Selenium get started docs
Sign up for a free trial account
Copy your Username and Access Key
Clone sample repo and run npm install
Set credentials in browserstack.yml config file
Run npm run sample-test to start build
View passing test results on Automate dashboard

name: Celonis
routeName: Free plan guided tour with demo data
firstSuccessOutcome: You open a pre-built process analysis on demo data inside the free Celonis Platform.

steps.action: Open Celonis Platform get started page
Sign up for a free Celonis account
Log in to the Celonis Platform
Start guided tour using bundled demo dataset
View the pre-built process analysis dashboard

name: Chronosphere
firstSuccessOutcome: The standalone Collector scrapes Prometheus metrics that stream into the Chronosphere Observability Platform.

steps.action: Open Chronosphere Collector get started docs
Obtain a Chronosphere tenant via onboarding
Create a service account and API token
Download Collector binary and make executable
Define config file with tenant endpoint
Run Collector with API token and gateway
Confirm metrics streaming into Observability Platform

name: Datadog
routeName: Linux host Agent install to first metrics
firstSuccessOutcome: The Datadog Agent reports host metrics that appear in the Datadog UI within minutes.

steps.action: Open Datadog Getting Started with the Agent
Create a free Datadog account
Get your Datadog API key
Run one-line Agent install script on Linux
Wait a few minutes for data upload
See host and metrics in Datadog UI

name: GitLab
routeName: Make your first Git commit tutorial
firstSuccessOutcome: Your first commit is pushed, merged, and visible in the GitLab project UI.

steps.action: Open Make your first Git commit tutorial
Create a GitLab.com account
Install Git on your local machine
Create SSH keys and add to GitLab
Create sample project initialized with README
Clone the repository to your machine
Create branch, edit README, stage the change
Commit and push branch to GitLab
Merge branch and view commit in GitLab

name: Honeycomb
firstSuccessOutcome: Your app's traces appear in the Honeycomb UI within seconds of making requests.

steps.action: Open Honeycomb Get Started with Traces
Sign up for a free Honeycomb account
Create and copy a Honeycomb API key
npm install OpenTelemetry Node.js SDK packages
Add tracing.js and otelconfig.yaml with API key
Run app with OTEL_CONFIG_FILE and tracing.js
Make a few requests to your service
See traces appear in the Honeycomb UI

name: Kong
routeName: Konnect quickstart: service, route, proxied request
firstSuccessOutcome: A request proxied through Kong Gateway returns a 200 from the upstream httpbin service.

steps.action: Open Kong Gateway get started docs
Sign up for a Kong Konnect account
Generate a Konnect personal access token
Run quickstart script to provision control/data plane
Create Gateway Service pointing to httpbin
Create Route on /mock for the Service
Curl the proxy URL and get 200

name: Microsoft Dynamics 365
firstSuccessOutcome: The trial app loads a dashboard populated with sample business data and a guided product tour.

steps.action: Open Start a Dynamics 365 free trial page
Select Try for free on an app tile
Enter or create a work account email
Provide contact info and credit card details
Wait for trial setup, then select Launch trial
View dashboards with sample data and tour

name: New Relic
firstSuccessOutcome: Your Node.js app reports transactions that appear in the New Relic APM UI.

steps.action: Open New Relic Node.js agent install docs
Sign up for a free New Relic account
Copy your New Relic license key
Run npm install newrelic in your app
Add newrelic.js config with license key
Start app with -r newrelic require flag
Generate some traffic to your app
Wait minutes, then see data in APM UI

name: NVIDIA Developer
firstSuccessOutcome: A CUDA sample compiles and runs, printing your GPU's properties with a PASS result.

steps.action: Open the CUDA Quick Start Guide docs
Download and install CUDA Toolkit for platform
Clone the NVIDIA cuda-samples repository
Build a sample such as deviceQuery
Run the compiled sample executable
See GPU device properties and PASS result

name: Sauce Labs
firstSuccessOutcome: A sample Selenium test runs on Sauce Labs and its results appear in the dashboard.

steps.action: Open Sauce Labs Selenium Quickstart docs
Sign up for a free trial account
Copy your Sauce Username and Access Key
Clone the sample example repository
Set credentials as environment variables
Run the sample Selenium WebDriver test
View test results on Sauce Labs dashboard

name: Terraform
firstSuccessOutcome: Terraform provisions an nginx Docker container reachable at localhost:8000.

steps.action: Open Terraform Docker Get Started tutorial
Install Terraform CLI and Docker locally
Create main.tf defining an nginx container
Run terraform init to install the provider
Run terraform apply and confirm with yes
Visit localhost:8000 to see nginx running
